bead.title: bench-pr-A1: harbor-runner Docker image + sha-labeled build script
bead.description:
Create the fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image that wraps Harbor and its Python adapters so the host stops needing Python.

FILES:
  - scripts/benchmark/Dockerfile.harbor-runner: FROM python:3.12-slim; pip install harbor-bench==<pinned>; COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_adapters/ /app/harbor_adapters/; COPY scripts/benchmark/harbor_agent.py /app/harbor_agent.py; ENV PYTHONPATH=/app; WORKDIR /app; ENTRYPOINT ["harbor"].
  - scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh: docker build with image-content-sha label = sha256 of harbor_adapters/ + harbor_agent.py + Dockerfile.harbor-runner.

SPIKE (do first, ~5 min): confirm `pip install harbor-bench==<pinned>` resolves; if Harbor is git-only, swap to `pip install git+https://github.com/laude-institute/...`. Record the resolved pin in a comment in the Dockerfile.

OUT OF SCOPE: the ./benchmark preflight subcommand that calls build-harbor-runner.sh (covered by child A3).

ACCEPTANCE:
  1. scripts/benchmark/build-harbor-runner.sh builds successfully and emits an image tagged fizeau-harbor-runner:latest with a non-empty image-content-sha label.
  2. docker run --rm fizeau-harbor-runner:latest --help exits 0 and prints Harbor CLI help.
  3. Re-running build-harbor-runner.sh without source changes reuses the cached image (label sha unchanged).
bead.acceptance: All 3 commands in the description ACCEPTANCE block pass.
